title: Umami Crunchy Japanese Pecan Nuts Ramen
description: A hearty, slow-cooked Japanese-inspired ramen featuring crunchy pecan nuts and a rich umami flavor profile.

ingredients.name: Pork bones
ingredients.notes: You can also use chicken bones or a combination of both.

ingredients.name: Soy sauce
ingredients.notes: Use a low-sodium soy sauce for a healthier option.

ingredients.name: Ginger
ingredients.notes: You can also use ground ginger if you prefer.

ingredients.name: Mushrooms
ingredients.notes: You can use any type of mushroom you like, such as shiitake or cremini.

ingredients.name: Noodles
ingredients.notes: Use Japanese-style ramen noodles for the best flavor.

instructions: Heat the sesame oil in a large pot over medium heat.
Add the ginger and garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the mushrooms and onion and cook for 5 minutes, until they start to soften.
Add the pork bones and cook for 5 minutes, until they start to brown.
Add the soy sauce and 4 cups of water to the pot.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 2 hours.
Add the pecan nuts to the pot and cook for an additional 30 minutes.
Cook the noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
To serve, place some noodles in a bowl, then add some of the hot broth and top with sliced green onions and additional pecan nuts if desired.
